slides.title: Objects that Perform Actions ⚙️
slides.content:
Objects aren't just for storing static data like strings and numbers; they often contain functions that operate on that data.

Functions stored inside objects are called **Methods**.

slides.title: Typing Methods
slides.content: You type a method in an interface by declaring its parameters and returning type right there in the object block.

quizData.question: What is the return type of the `bootUp` method in the interface above?
quizData.explanation: The interface dictates that `bootUp` must return `void` (nothing).

slides.title: Implementing the Logic
slides.content: Now when we create an object following that interface, we actually write the function logic:

slides.title: Arrow Function Syntax 🏹
slides.content:
You will frequently see method typing written using sleek arrow syntax instead:
`bootUp: (msg?: string) => void;`
Both are perfectly valid approaches when building interfaces!
